title: UXC Limited - 2011-12 return of capital
description: Find out the tax consequences for shareholders who received a return of capital on their UXC shares.
keywords: UXC, return of capital, capital return, cost base adjustment, non-assessable payments, UXC shares

content_blocks.text: This information applies to you if:

items: you are an individual, not a company or trust
you held shares in UXC and received the return of capital in December 2011
you acquired your shares under an employee share scheme (ESS) and have been taxed on the discount amount under the ESS provisions
any gain or loss you made on the shares is a capital gain or capital loss - this means that you held your shares as an investment asset, not
as trading stock as part of carrying on a business, or to make a short-term or 'one-off' commercial gain.

content_blocks.text: All shareholders who held UXC shares at 7pm on 2 December 2011 (record date) were entitled to receive the return of capital.
The payment date of the return of capital was 7 December 2011.
The return of capital was $0.02 per share. The payment was a capital payment. It was not a dividend for any purpose and had no dividend component.
There are two tax consequences. You need to:

items: work out whether you have made a capital gain by comparing the cost base of your shares with the return of capital you received
adjust the cost base (and reduced cost base) of any UXC shares you owned on 7 December 2011.

content_text:
This information applies to you if:
- you are an individual, not a company or trust
- you held shares in UXC and received the return of capital in December 2011
- you acquired your shares under an employee share scheme (ESS) and have been taxed on the discount amount under the ESS provisions
- any gain or loss you made on the shares is a capital gain or capital loss - this means that you held your shares as an investment asset, not
- as trading stock as part of carrying on a business, or to make a short-term or 'one-off' commercial gain.
## Return of capital
All shareholders who held UXC shares at 7pm on 2 December 2011 (record date) were entitled to receive the return of capital.
The payment date of the return of capital was 7 December 2011.
The return of capital was $0.02 per share. The payment was a capital payment. It was not a dividend for any purpose and had no dividend component.
### Tax consequences
There are two tax consequences. You need to:
- work out whether you have made a capital gain by comparing the cost base of your shares with the return of capital you received
- adjust the cost base (and reduced cost base) of any UXC shares you owned on 7 December 2011.

child_links.title: Capital gains tax (CGT) implications
child_links.description: A CGT event happened on 7 December 2011 when UXC paid a return of capital on your UXC shares.

child_links.title: If you owned shares when you received the return of capital on 7 December 2011
child_links.description: If you owned shares when you received the return of capital on 7 December 2011

child_links.title: If you sold your shares just before receiving the return of capital on 7 December 2011
child_links.description: A CGT event happened when you sold your UXC shares.

child_links.title: More information
child_links.description: For more information about this return of capital.
